ddt:Target.Name: AMD_Cluster_v2.2
ddt:Target.Description:
07-09-2022 Author: Akhil S V2.21 -Diversity for EgocarType added DID$0134 -Vehicle Project xDE added Newly -New DIDs $015C,$015D,$015E -DIDs Updated $0114,$0157,$0372,$0126,$010E,$0361 -DTC added $E176,E18A -Remove DID $0359 -$19 Request Response Corrected Mega frame Update V5 Based on following specs Diag-Resource_Config_Status_RoutineC1AHSEVO_AMDC_Config_Status_Routine_SW4072 [Diag-Resource_DTC_IO_Ident]Cluster_SWEET400_SWEET100Reg_v9
13-05-2022 Author: Akhil V2.1 Added applicability for XBGph² and removed applicability XGB Megaframe Updatae size and parameter,DID Position & mega frame Screen new DID added $0157,$0158,$0159,$015A,$015B. Deleted DTC $9407,$9415 15,$940B Deleted DID $0335,$0140 DTC Code update $C10B to $C10C, $E16F to $C10D Corrected spelling of DTC $9404,$9408 Corrected spelling of DID $0102 Bit Size Corrected $013A Coding Parameter Added/ Corrected $2009,$0111 Resolution Corrected $2102, $2106, $2107 Screen Update $2701,$2703 and Screen Colour added for Read and Write ,New Screen add for Cluster diagnosis & TCI Reset capitalization,ClusterUserSetMenu_p1 & p2 Mnemonics Corrected for DIDs Snapshot DID added $020B-I/o Control $7006 Control State update Based on Diag-Resource_Config_Status_RoutineC1AHSEVO_AMDC_Config_Status_Routine_SW4072 [MegaFr]Cluster_MegaFrame_Specification_v4 [Diag-Resource_DTC_IO_Ident]Cluster_SWEET400_SWEET100Reg_v8
23-02-2021 Author: Julien R V2.0 - Added DID : $010A - Deleted DID : $011A: ChronotachographPresence_CF - Corrected spelling of DID "L2DisplayedXX_CF" ("D" instead of "d" after "L2") - Corrected spelling of DID $20F1/$20F2/$210B/$210C/$240A/$240C/$240D/$2410 - Corrected spelling of WidgetPresenceMMI_CF.BICARB_SECOND_GAUGE and ADACWorldPresence_CF.Data_Icon and TelltaleAutotestP1_CF.lightingFailure - Spelling of DID ClusterUserSetMenu_pX_CF changed to ClusterUserSetMenu_PX_CF to ensure consistency with GCC - All previous and current auto-idents gathered in this database (previous database versions will be deleted from central) - Updated $7006 routine - Added applicability for XJB²
23-12-2021 Author: Akhil V1.3 - Updated DID $0154 $011A $0137 $0113 $0114 $0365 $0366 $0136 $0119 $0361 $280B - Updated DTC $C10A $C10B $9429 $9407 - Newly added DTC $9407 $C106 $C1067 $C108 $C109 $9431 $9432 $9433 $9434 $9435 $9436 $F0D3 - Added auto-ident for S100 + S400 PI6 Sp2 SW Based on - Diag-Resource_Config_Status_Routine]C1AREG_AMDC_Config_Status_Routine_SW1053 & [Diag-Resource_Config_Status_Routine]C1AREG_AMDC_Config_Status_Routine_SW4053 - Diag-Resource_DTC_IO_Ident]Cluster_SWEET400_SWEET100Reg_v7
25-10-2021 Authors: Julien R / Akhil V1.2 DID - Updated DID:$013F,$0365,$0366,$0121,$0137,$0119,$0113,$0114,$0372,$240A,$240B - Deleted DID:$0115 $0117 $0118 $011B $011C $0128 $0132 $013E $0142 $0145 $0146 $014A $014C $014E $014F $0150 $0151 $032C $032D $032E $032F $0330 $0331 $0337 $0358 $0360 $F100 - Newly added DID: $0148, $0149, $0152 - Correction of DID $2803 : resolution is now -400 (instead of 0) - All 1-bit size DIDs : corrected bit offset from 0 to 7 (active bit is coded on LSB, not MSB anymore) - DID $0365 & $0356 : corrected text values to see ECU/feature names in screens (instead of only present/absent) Based on following specs : - C1AReg_AMDC_Config_Status_Routine_SW1045 - Cluster_SWEET400_SWEET100Reg_v6 Updated AutoIdent for PI5 SW
16-08-2021 Author: Soumya V1.1 Megaframe update and DID updates-$0135,$2406,$2802,$2805,$2806,$0126,$2408 DID add- $2816,$2817,$2818,$2819,$2812,$2813,$2814,$2815 DTC remove-$9431, $9432, $9433,$9435,$9436,$C118,$C122,$C243, $E10B, $E113, $E13D,$E164, $E16D,$E175,$E176 DTC add- $C104,$C105, $E19B, $E171,$E16F [Diag-Resource_SW4042, [Diag-Resource_DTC_IO_Ident]Cluster_SWEET400_SWEET100Reg_v5 11-03-2021 Author: Soumya V1.0 Creation: Diag-Resource_Config_Status_Routine_C1AHS_12i and [Diag-Resource_DTC_IO_Ident]Cluster_SWEET400_v2 ,[Diag-Resource_DTC_IO_Ident]Cluster_SWEET400_SWEET100Reg_v3

Item.Text: Device and failure type ODB codding
circuit short to ground or open
circuit short to battery or open
signal rate of change below threshold
signal rate of change above threshold
signal bias level out of range / zero adjt failure
signal has too few transitions / events
signal has too many transitions / events
alive / sequence counter incorrect / not updated
value of signal protection calculation incorrect
Seat belt telltale command monitoring (external telltale)
High Side PWM outputs for backlight monitoring

Data.Name: DTCExtendedData.Mileage
Data.Comment: Description of the extendedData Mileage (use by Failure Page to decode data)

Data.Name: DTCExtendedData.AgingCounter
Data.Comment: Description of the extendedData AgingCounter (use by Failure Page to decode data)

Data.Name: DTCExtendedData.DTCOccurrenceCounter
Data.Comment: Description of the extendedData DTCOccurenceCounter (use by Failure Page to decode data)

Data.Name: OutputControlStatus
Data.Comment: Testing output : Status

Data.Name: VIN
Data.Comment: VINDataIdentifier

Data.Name: Language_CF
Data.Comment: This parameter allows identifying the language used for messages displayed.

Data.Name: CarburationType_CF
Data.Comment: This parameter allows identifying the carburation type of the vehicle.

Data.Name: DistanceUnit_CF
Data.Comment: This parameter allows identifying the distance unit type (km or miles).

Data.Name: VolumeUnit_CF
Data.Comment: This parameter allows identifying the volume unit of the vehicle.

Data.Name: TripComputerType_CF
Data.Comment: This parameter allows identifying the trip computer type displayed.

Data.Name: TyrePressureUnit_CF
Data.Comment: This parameter allows identifying the tyre pressure unit displayed.

Data.Name: ParkingBrakeType_CF
Data.Comment: This parameter allows identifying the parking brake type (manual or assisted).

Data.Name: OverspeedWarningPresent_CF
Data.Comment: This parameter allows identifying the presence of overspeed warning (10204_1).

Data.Name: VolumeFuelWarning_CF
Data.Comment: used to define the threshold below low fuel level warning occurs.

Data.Name: TripDistanceKm
Data.Comment: This parameter is used for trip computer calculations.

Data.Name: TripFuelUsed
Data.Comment: This parameter is used for trip computer calculations.

Data.Name: TripTimer
Data.Comment: This parameter is used for trip computer calculations.

Data.Name: L0Value
Data.Comment: This parameter is the measure of fuel gauge sensor converted in litre using Vx_FUNC_Rx table.

Data.Name: HandBrakeState
Data.Comment: Physical Input

Data.Name: BrakeFluidLevelState
Data.Comment: Physical Input

Item.Text: Circuit short to battery or open circuit

Data.Name: FuelGaugeValue
Data.Comment: Physical Input
Data.Description: --> If OC then FuelGaugeValue = V0FuncR0.Resistance_CF --> If SC then FuelGaugeValue = V10FuncR10.Resistance_CF --> If voltage is out of range then FuelGaugeValue = $FFFF

Data.Name: BatteryVoltageMeasurement
Data.Comment: Physical Input

Item.Text: Out of range (measured resistance doesn't match with defined resistance ranges)

Data.Name: UPAMMI_CF
Data.Comment: parameter allows identifying the presence of UPA Ultrasonic Park Assist display & sound in Cluster.

Data.Name: TPMSMMI_CF
Data.Comment: This parameter allows identifying the presence of TPMS or TPW display in cluster

Data.Name: ESCMMI_CF
Data.Comment: This parameter is used for ESC message filtering(only 20403_4)

Data.Name: ExternalTemperature_CF
Data.Comment: This parameter allows identifying the external temperature management.
Data.Description: Note 1: When this parameter is at 01b, it means that External Temperature sensor acquisition is not managed directly by the cluster

Data.Name: TemperatureUnit_CF
Data.Comment: Defines the default temperature unit that must be displayed by the Cluster ECU

Data.Name: DimmingStrategy_CF
Data.Comment: This parameter allows to define which kind of dimmer is used in the vehicle

Data.Name: NivocodSensorType_CF
Data.Comment: whether or not brake fluid level sensor has // resistor to distinguish normaly open circ vs no senso

Item.Text: Without R// or DIAG by CAN

Data.Name: MSCOilChgInitialDistanceInKM_CF
Data.Comment: Shall be written together with MSCOilChangeUsualDistanceInKM !

Data.Name: MSCOilChgInitialPeriodInDay_CF
Data.Comment: Shall be written together with MSCOilChangeUsualPeriodInDay !

Data.Name: MSCOilChgUsualDistanceInKM
Data.Comment: Is the current value displayed for the Oil Schedule Change in km.

Data.Name: FrontSBRInhibition_CF
Data.Comment: This parameter allows the activation or not of sound only for Front SBR warning (10104,10105)

Data.Name: CauseOfLastTripComputerReset
Data.Comment: CauseOfLastTripComputerRese

Item.Text: Overflow of Electrical consumption value (EV specific)

Data.Comment: systemSupplierECUSoftwareNumberDataIdentifier
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the vehicle manufacturer spare part number.

Record data content and format shall be server specific and defined by the vehicle manufacturer.

Data.Name: ECUSerialNumber
Data.Comment: ECUSerialNumberDataIdentifier
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the ECU (server) serial number.

Record data content and format shall be server specific.

Data.Comment: This configuration is used to activate or deactivate the welcome sequence

Data.Comment: identificationOptionVehicleManufacturerSpecific

Data.Comment: Routine information

Data.Name: OperationalReference
Data.Comment: OperationalReference

Data.Name: VehicleGUIStyle_CF
Data.Comment: Allows to change the representation of the vehicle in the graphism shown in the TFT

Data.Name: TACStrategy_CF
Data.Comment: This parameter allows to select which filtering strategy of RPM pointer shall be used.

Data.Name: WelcomeSequenceType_CF
Data.Comment: This parameter allows identifying the welcome sequence variants (dependant on IVI generation)

Data.Name: OilLevelPresent_CF
Data.Comment: Allows identifying the presence of oil level management (10102_x) and DTC management

Data.Name: MSCOilChgWarningThresholdKM_CF
Data.Comment: This configuration is used to define the first warning threshold for maintenance schedule in kilomet
Data.Description: This configuration is used to define the first warning threshold for maintenance schedule in kilometer (ValConfigSeuilAlerte1_Km in 65450-2009-45-CDC_CLUSTER_X98).

Data.Name: MSCOilDistanceCounterPresent_CF
Data.Comment: allows to define if Draining distance counter is displayed on Cluster and value computation strategy

Item.Text: Present with OCS AND cluster internal counter

Data.Name: MSCOilTimeCounterPresent_CF
Data.Comment: This parameter allows to define if Draining timer is displayed

Data.Name: MSCServDistanceCounterPresent_CF
Data.Comment: This parameter allows to define if Service distance counter is displayed

Data.Name: MSCServTimeCounterPresent_CF
Data.Comment: This parameter allows to define if Service timer is displayed

Data.Name: MSCServiceWarningThresholdKM_CF
Data.Comment: Used to define the first warning threshold for Service schedule in kilometer.
Data.Description: This configuration is used to define the first warning threshold for maintenance schedule in kilometer (ValConfigSeuilAlerte1_Km in 65450-2009-45-CDC_CLUSTER_X98).

Data.Name: FuelRangeCorrectKmAutonom_CF
Data.Comment: Define what t is the distance range on which the driving range is computed. Cf. Data doc field
Data.Description: Used for defining what is the distance range on which the driving range is computed. If value is 255 then the computation is done with the trip computer average consumption. Note: not used for bicarburation

Data.Name: TCI_RemainingDistanceIncreaseInhibitionThreshold_CF
Data.Description:
If bargraph display, bar 9 will light on if fuel level calculated is > L2_9.

If pointer display, pointer must be set at 8/8 position if fuel level calculated = L2_9.

Data.Name: TCI_RemainingDistanceWarningThreshold_CF
Data.Description:
If bargraph display, bar 9 will light on if fuel level calculated is > L2_9.

If pointer display, pointer must be set at 8/8 position if fuel level calculated = L2_9.

Data.Name: MexType_CF
Data.Comment: Used to identifying the management of the current driving mode

Data.Name: SCRMMI_CF
Data.Comment: Used to display SCR functions

Data.Name: S1Kmin_CF
Data.Comment: parameter is used as a weighting (usually <1) to the fuel consumption for L2 computation.

Data.Name: S1Kmaj_CF
Data.Comment: parameter is used as a weighting (usually >1) to the fuel consumption for L2 computation.

Data.Name: S2Kmaj_CF
Data.Comment: parameter is used as a weighting (usually >1) to the fuel consumption for L2 computation.

Data.Name: S3Kmaj_CF
Data.Comment: parameter is used as a weighting (usually >1) to the fuel consumption for L2 computation.

Data.Name: TripFuelLPG
Data.Comment: Used for LPG trip computer calculations

Data.Name: TripFuelCNG
Data.Comment: Used for CNG trip computer calculations

Data.Name: TripDistLPGCNG
Data.Comment: Used for trip computer calculations

Data.Name: L3DuringFuelReadjust
Data.Comment: This parameter keeps the value present during the last fuel update what
Data.Description: This parameter keeps the value present during the last fuel update whatever the fuel update cause.

Data.Name: L2offDuringFuelReadjust
Data.Comment: This parameter keeps the value present during the last fuel update w
Data.Description: This parameter keeps the value present during the last fuel update whatever the fuel update cause.

Data.Name: L0onDuringFuelReadjust
Data.Comment: This parameter keeps the value present during the last fuel update wh
Data.Description: This parameter keeps the value present during the last fuel update whatever the fuel update cause.

Data.Name: DimmerResistance
Data.Comment: physical input

Data.Name: ExtECUDistBeforeReadjust
Data.Comment: The cluster stored distance before readjustment (in case of CAN incoherence or Cluster incoherence)
Data.Description: The cluster stored distance before readjustment (in case of CAN incoherence or Cluster incoherence, see EN_ODO_036 and EN_ODO_040). The stored distance is the value of CAN received parameter before readjusting.

Data.Name: IGNWire
Data.Comment: Status of the ignition wire

Item.Text: Out of range (measured resistance doesn't match with defined resistance range)

Data.Name: EgocarType_CF
Data.Comment: Used to display the ego car according to the vehicle type

Item.Text: DO NOT USE Phantom PV without badging
DO NOT USE Phantom PV Renault
DO NOT USE Phantom PV Dacia
DO NOT USE Phantom PV Lada
DO NOT USE Phantom PV Nissan
DO NOT USE Phantom PV Alpine
DO NOT USE Phantom LCV without badging
DO NOT USE Phantom LCV Renault
DO NOT USE Phantom LCV Dacia
DO NOT USE Phantom LCV Lada
DO NOT USE Phantom LCV Nissan

Data.Name: vehicleManufacturerECUSoftwareNumber
Data.Comment: vehicleManufacturerECUSoftwareNumber

Data.Name: vehicleManufacturerSparePartNumber
Data.Comment: Ordered Part Number - This value shall contain the number of the part ordered by OEM
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the vehicle manufacturer spare part number.

Record data content and format shall be server specific and defined by the vehicle manufacturer.

Data.Name: DTCCount
Data.Comment: DTC count

Data.Name: BootVersion
Data.Comment: BootSoftwareIdentificationDataIdentifier
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the vehicle manufacturer spare part number.

Record data content and format shall be server specific and defined by the vehicle manufacturer.

Data.Name: ClusterUserSetPresent_CF
Data.Comment: Allows identifying the presence of User setting menu in the cluster

Data.Name: DIM_OutputWhenDayNightOnly_CF
Data.Comment: This parameter allows to define backlight level during night in Day/Night mode

Data.Name: DPFManualRegenMMI_CF
Data.Comment: This parameter allows to activate DPF regen procedure page in the ADAC loop

Data.Name: DIM_DayNightModifiedByPCFN_CF
Data.Comment: This configuration used to force dimming off when PCFN/DisplayRequest =0.

Data.Name: DTCFormatIdentifier
Data.Comment: DTC format - Renault/Nissan use value 0 (3 bytes DTC)

Data.Name: ClusterDistBeforeReadjust
Data.Comment: Cluster stores cluster current distance before readjustement (in case of CAN or cluster incoherence)
Data.Description: The cluster stores cluster current distance before readjustement (in case of CAN incoherence or Cluster incoherence, see EN_ODO_036 and EN_ODO_040). The stored distance is the last value of cluster_current_distance calculated parameter before readjusting.

Data.Name: ClockDisplayMode_CF
Data.Comment: This parameter allows to display the clock info in different format dpending on market requirements.

Data.Name: Clock_CF
Data.Comment: Allows identifying the display AND management (includes setting) of the clock (CodingInstructions!)

Item.Text: Clock Displayed from CAN / RTC OFF
Clock displayed / Not set / RTC ON
Clock displayed and set / RTC ON
Clock display and set / RTC in Radio

Data.Name: ChargingPlugTelltaleVariant_CF
Data.Comment: This parameter allows to deal with the charging plug telltale color diversity (China regulation)

Data.Name: ATBeepInR_CF
Data.Comment: Used to allow to emit a sound in case of AT switched to R.

Item.Text: Overflow of electrical consumption value (EV specific)
Overflow of electrical consumption value (EV specific)

Data.Name: OutputControl_Buttons.Value
Data.Comment: This service is able to manage permanently pointer(s) driven by the microcontroller.

Data.Name: OutputControl_Display.Value
Data.Comment: This service is able to manage permanently the display(s) driven by the microcontroller.

Item.Text: Main Display Black image (TFT + backlight ON)
Main display - All segments ON (if LCD) or white for TFT
Main display - All pixels ON color 1 (Design_Red)
Main display - All pixels ON color 2 (Design_Green)
Main display - All pixels ON color 3 (Design_Blue)
Main display - inverted chess pattern ON
Main display All pixels ON color Design_Jaune
Main display All pixels ON color Design_CyanFonce
Main display All pixels ON color Design_Rose
Main display All pixels ON color Design_Orange
Main display All pixels ON color Design_BleuCiel
Main display All pixels ON color Design_Violet
Main display All pixels ON color Design_NavyBlue
Main display All pixels ON color Design_Vertdeau
Main display All pixels ON color Design_Orangefonce
Main display All pixels ON color Design_RougeLight

Data.Name: OutputControl_Backlight.Value
Data.Comment: This service is able to manage permanently the backlight(s) driven by the microcontroller.

Item.Text: All pointers are at rest position

Data.Name: Supply voltage
Data.Comment: Physical Input

Data.Name: GAU_TempoForVU_CF
Data.Comment: Used to adapt fuel stabilization temporization to LCV/CDV (big fuel tanks)

Data.Name: LastKilometersFuel
Data.Comment: Fuel autonomy = (L2 value*FuelRangeCorrectKmAutonom_CF)/LastKilometersFuel

Data.Name: I_BoosterSafetybag_CF
Data.Comment: Used to inhibite the safetybag function for I-booster, function present in some EV/PHEV/HEV vehicles

Data.Name: EgocarColor_CF
Data.Comment: This configuration is used to defined egocar color when egocar feature is not managed by calibration

Data.Name: Node_ECUPresent_Part1_CF.NodeETC_4GRIP
Data.Comment: This parameter allows identifying the presence of ECU. Used for ECU frame lost & ErrorProg

Data.Name: LDWMMI_CF
Data.Comment: used to defined if buzzer shall be played by cluster or not when LDW crossing alert occurs

Data.Name: CauseOfLastStopService.BUIC line number related to last stop alert
Data.Comment: BUIC line number related to last stop alert (MBD parameter : CauseStop)

Data.Name: CauseOfLastStopService.BUIC line number related to last service alert
Data.Comment: BUIC line number related to last service alert (MBD parameter : CauseService)

Data.Name: RadioDisplayManagedInCluster_CF
Data.Comment: Mgmt of the radio display in the cluster depending on R020 or A-IVI presence on CAN M

Data.Name: GaugingThresholdLow_CF
Data.Comment: This configuration parameter is used to select the Kmin/Kmaj parameters for the "low level":

Data.Name: FuelReadjustL2offL0_CF
Data.Comment: This configuration is used as threshold for L2offL0 readjustement(big gap):

Data.Name: FuelReadjustNearFullL2_CF
Data.Comment: parameter is used to define the near full area and select the associated readjustement mode

Data.Name: V0FuncR0Volume_CF
Data.Comment: This configuration define a Volume used in the conversation Resistance to Volume.

Data.Name: V0FuncR0Resistance_CF
Data.Comment: V0FuncR0 is the bottom of the tank

Data.Name: V1FuncR1Volume_CF
Data.Comment: This configuration define a Volume used in the conversation Resistance to Volume.

Data.Name: V1FuncR1Resistance_CF
Data.Comment: This configuration define a Resistance used in the conversation Resistance to Volume

Data.Name: V2FuncR2Resistance_CF
Data.Comment: This configuration define a Resistance used in the conversation Resistance to Volume

Data.Name: MSCOil_DistanceForcedTarget
Data.Comment: MSCOil_DistanceForcedTarget = current odometer + distance before draining
Data.Description: This value is the odometer target when oil change is due. This value cannot be less than current odometer value. MSCOil_DistanceForcedTarget = current odometer + distance before draining. The main usage of this DID is for plant in order to write and read same value (ie: MSCOil_DistanceForcedTarget = 0 + first draining interval) . In after sale, previous routine and DID are still applicable

Data.Name: RoutineIdentifierList
Data.Comment: Routine identifier - This is an example. See documentation for more information.
Data.Description:
Definition of RoutineControl

Data: RoutineIdentifierList

  Contains the list of all routine identifier that may be used with InputOutputControl service.
 
  Example:
  
    0x4501 - ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave

If some of these routine identifer have parameters, you should defined a data with the same name as the routine identifer followed by a dot and a text

  Example:
 
  Data: ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave.Min
  Data: ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave.Max
  Data: ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave.Freq

Then, you should add theses data in the "Send tab" of the RoutineControl request, after the RoutineIdentifierList data.

Data.Name: ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave.Min
Data.Comment: This is an example data for RoutineControl request. See RoutineIdentiifierList documentation.

Data.Name: ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave.Max
Data.Comment: This is an example data for RoutineControl request. See RoutineIdentiifierList documentation.

Data.Comment: This configuration is define the position of the gauge on display according the L2 value
Data.Description:
If bargraph display, bar 9 will light on if fuel level calculated is > L2_9.

If pointer display, pointer must be set at 8/8 position if fuel level calculated = L2_9.

Data.Name: MemorizedOILMeasures
Data.Comment: Used for reading oil level

Data.Name: MegaFrame1.EE_Architecture_Type_CF
Data.Comment: allows to filter some Cluster frame emission when several EE architecture are adressed with the same

Data.Name: MegaFrame1.HMI_InteractionMode_CF
Data.Comment: Layout mode shall be used in front a IVI and else use World mode.

Data.Name: MegaFrame1.L2Displayed1_CF
Data.Description: If bargraph display, bar 1 will flash is fuel level calculated is ? L2_1. If pointer display, pointer must be set at 0/8 position (lower limit in the red zone _ Reserve fuel side) if fuel level calculated = L2_1.

Data.Name: MegaFrame1.L2Displayed10_CF
Data.Description:
If bargraph display, bar 9 will light on if fuel level calculated is > L2_9.

If pointer display, pointer must be set at 8/8 position if fuel level calculated = L2_9.

Data.Name: MegaFrame1.L2Displayed11_CF
Data.Description:
If bargraph display, bar 9 will light on if fuel level calculated is > L2_9.

If pointer display, pointer must be set at 8/8 position if fuel level calculated = L2_9.

Data.Name: MegaFrame1.L2Displayed12_CF
Data.Description:
If bargraph display, bar 9 will light on if fuel level calculated is > L2_9.

If pointer display, pointer must be set at 8/8 position if fuel level calculated = L2_9.

Data.Name: MegaFrame1.L2Displayed2_CF
Data.Description:
If bargraph display, bar 2 will light on if fuel level calculated is > L2_2.

If pointer display, pointer must be set at 1/8 position if fuel level calculated = L2_2.

Data.Name: MegaFrame1.L2Displayed3_CF
Data.Description:
If bargraph display, bar 3 will light on if fuel level calculated is > L2_3.

If pointer display, pointer must be set at 2/8 position if fuel level calculated = L2_3.

Data.Name: MegaFrame1.L2Displayed4_CF
Data.Description:
If bargraph display, bar 4 will light on if fuel level calculated is > L2_4.

If pointer display, pointer must be set at 3/8 position if fuel level calculated = L2_4.

Data.Name: MegaFrame1.L2Displayed5_CF
Data.Description:
If bargraph display, bar 5 will light on if fuel level calculated is > L2_5.

If pointer display, pointer must be set at 4/8 position if fuel level calculated = L2_5.

Data.Name: MegaFrame1.L2Displayed6_CF
Data.Description:
If bargraph display, bar 6 will light on if fuel level calculated is > L2_6.

If pointer display, pointer must be set at 5/8 position if fuel level calculated = L2_6.

Data.Name: MegaFrame1.L2Displayed7_CF
Data.Description:
If bargraph display, bar 7 will light on if fuel level calculated is > L2_7.

If pointer display, pointer must be set at 6/8 position if fuel level calculated = L2_7.

Data.Name: MegaFrame1.L2Displayed8_CF
Data.Description:
If bargraph display, bar 8 will light on if fuel level calculated is > L2_8.

If pointer display, pointer must be set at 7/8 position if fuel level calculated = L2_8.

Data.Name: MegaFrame1.L2Displayed9_CF
Data.Description:
If bargraph display, bar 9 will light on if fuel level calculated is > L2_9.

If pointer display, pointer must be set at 8/8 position if fuel level calculated = L2_9.
